text.content: Markets are driven by the mechanics of the order book, not just sentiment. In crypto, price movements are often accelerated by

rich_text.plain_text: Markets are driven by the mechanics of the order book, not just sentiment. In crypto, price movements are often accelerated by

text.content: When price hits a cluster of liquidation levels, the exchange is forced to execute automated "Market Sell" or "Market Buy" orders. This creates a feedback loop: one liquidation triggers the next, causing the price to "teleport" much further and faster than it would during organic trading.

rich_text.plain_text: When price hits a cluster of liquidation levels, the exchange is forced to execute automated "Market Sell" or "Market Buy" orders. This creates a feedback loop: one liquidation triggers the next, causing the price to "teleport" much further and faster than it would during organic trading.

text.content: Professional liquidity providers, or Market Makers, aim to stay delta-neutral. When a cascade starts, they recognize "toxic flow"—one-sided, forced orders that increase their inventory risk.

rich_text.plain_text: Professional liquidity providers, or Market Makers, aim to stay delta-neutral. When a cascade starts, they recognize "toxic flow"—one-sided, forced orders that increase their inventory risk.

text.content: To protect themselves, they often "quote wider" or pull their limit orders entirely. This sudden removal of liquidity is why price seems to "slip" through levels without any meaningful resistance until the cascade is exhausted.

rich_text.plain_text: To protect themselves, they often "quote wider" or pull their limit orders entirely. This sudden removal of liquidity is why price seems to "slip" through levels without any meaningful resistance until the cascade is exhausted.

wiki.hook: One Bollinger Band shows volatility on one timeframe. This stacks them across multiple timeframes simultaneously so you see the squeeze before it fires.

text.content: The Bollinger MTF indicator is a versatile volatility and trend analysis tool that allows traders to overlay Bollinger Bands from multiple timeframes onto a single chart. This provides a comprehensive view of market structure, identifying key support and resistance levels across various durations—from intraday scalping ranges to long-term institutional cycles.

education.title: Liquidity and Market Makers
education.summary: Liquidity cascades are self-reinforcing cycles where price movements trigger a wave of forced orders, which in turn drive the price further in the same directio...

text.content: Liquidity cascades are self-reinforcing cycles where price movements trigger a wave of forced orders, which in turn drive the price further in the same direction, triggering even more orders. In crypto, this is most commonly seen during "long squeezes" or "short squeezes."

rich_text.plain_text: Liquidity cascades are self-reinforcing cycles where price movements trigger a wave of forced orders, which in turn drive the price further in the same direction, triggering even more orders. In crypto, this is most commonly seen during "long squeezes" or "short squeezes."

text.content: The process functions as a feedback loop. For example, in a downward cascade:

rich_text.plain_text: The process functions as a feedback loop. For example, in a downward cascade:

text.content: Price drops to a level where a cluster of "Long" positions have their liquidation prices.

rich_text.plain_text: Price drops to a level where a cluster of "Long" positions have their liquidation prices.

text.content: The exchange forcibly closes these positions. A long liquidation is essentially a "Market Sell" order.

rich_text.plain_text: The exchange forcibly closes these positions. A long liquidation is essentially a "Market Sell" order.

text.content: Because these are urgent market orders, they consume the available "Limit Buy" orders in the book.

rich_text.plain_text: Because these are urgent market orders, they consume the available "Limit Buy" orders in the book.

text.content: This sudden selling pressure drives the price down further, hitting the liquidation prices of the next layer of long positions.

rich_text.plain_text: This sudden selling pressure drives the price down further, hitting the liquidation prices of the next layer of long positions.

text.content: This cycle repeats rapidly (cascades), causing price to "teleport" or move much further than it would under normal organic trading.

text.content: Market makers, recognizing the toxicity of a one-sided cascade, will often "quote wider" or pull their limit orders entirely to avoid getting steamrolled, which makes the price drop even faster due to a lack of support.

text.content: Market makers are professional liquidity providers. Their primary role is to ensure there is always a "Bid" and an "Ask" available for other traders to interact with.

rich_text.plain_text: Market makers are professional liquidity providers. Their primary role is to ensure there is always a "Bid" and an "Ask" available for other traders to interact with.

text.content: How They Make Money: The Spread

rich_text.plain_text: How They Make Money: The Spread

text.content: Market makers don't typically bet on whether the price goes up or down. Instead, they "capture the spread." They post a limit order to buy (the Bid) and a limit order to sell (the Ask). Their profit is the difference between these two prices, provided they can flip their "inventory" quickly.
